headline: World Governments Agree to Solve Climate Change With Mandatory Nap Time
summary: Global leaders believe less movement equals less carbon.
fullBody: In an emergency session, world leaders signed the International Siesta Accord. All citizens must nap for 2 hours daily. 'When we're sleeping, we're not driving or arguing,' explained one exhausted diplomat. Early results show reduced emissions but a dramatic increase in dream-based conspiracy theories.
